text.title: Rust Ownership Model Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed in real rust-ownership deployments. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + fix.

antipattern.symptom: .clone() on String / Vec sprinkled to make the code compile; profiler shows hot allocation.
antipattern.root-cause: Author cloned to silence the borrow checker rather than fix the design.
antipattern.fix: Lift the value into a longer-lived scope or borrow; document required clones.

antipattern.symptom: Rc<RefCell<State>> moved into tokio::spawn; compile error or runtime panic.
fn x(&self) -> &T; consumers fight lifetime errors at call sites.
antipattern.root-cause: Author relied on elision for non-trivial signatures.
antipattern.fix: Make lifetimes explicit when returning references; document the relationship.
